title: Sign Language Recognition
description: Real-time computer vision system using camera input to interpret gestures and generate structured output.
contributionNote: Built the complete pipeline from camera capture to structured output generation.

caseStudy.problem: Create a low-latency system that can convert sign gestures into readable text in real time.
caseStudy.goal: Improve communication accessibility through practical live gesture interpretation.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Camera stream capture -> landmark extraction -> gesture interpretation engine -> text output layer.
workflow: Normalized incoming frames for stable landmark detection.
Tracked hand and upper-body landmarks in real time.
Mapped landmark transitions to structured language tokens.
Added confidence filtering to reduce false predictions.
caseStudy.results: Delivered stable gesture-to-text output in live tests with practical response speed.
imageNotes: The project image shows the primary capture and analysis state used to validate detection consistency.

title: 3D Anatomy
description: Interactive web-based anatomy platform with gender toggle and modular structure.
contributionNote: Implemented modular UI architecture and interactive navigation logic.

caseStudy.problem: Users needed a clear way to explore complex anatomical structures in-browser without getting lost in 3D space.
caseStudy.goal: Provide a structured learning experience that keeps detail high but navigation simple.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: React UI state controls scene filters and model groups rendered by Three.js.
workflow: Built modular panels for model categories and structure details.
Integrated interactive model controls and progressive reveal behavior.
Aligned camera interaction with UI state to preserve orientation context.
caseStudy.results: Produced a responsive anatomy explorer with smooth interaction and clear user flow.
imageNotes: The screenshot captures the integrated UI and 3D scene interaction model.

title: Baymax
description: Mental health tracking system focused on mood logging and user-centered interaction.
contributionNote: Designed the user flow for mood tracking and support-oriented interaction.

caseStudy.problem: Mood-tracking apps often lose users because repeated entry flows are too heavy.
caseStudy.goal: Build a lightweight and empathetic interaction flow for daily mood check-ins.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Mobile mood input and timeline UI connected to Node.js event logic.
workflow: Designed low-friction daily logging screens.
Structured mood events for trend aggregation and review.
Connected response logic to recent emotional patterns.
caseStudy.results: Created a practical emotional tracking prototype with consistent interaction behavior.
imageNotes: The image represents the primary user flow where mood input and support prompts are combined.

title: Telegram Bot
description: Automation system with event-based command handling and real-time responses.
contributionNote: Built command routing and stable real-time response handling.

caseStudy.problem: Manual handling of repetitive chat tasks was slow and error-prone.
caseStudy.goal: Automate command workflows with predictable and fast responses.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Incoming events are parsed by a command router and dispatched to modular handlers.
workflow: Defined a command map and standardized input parsing.
Implemented handler modules with fallback responses.
Added robust behavior for malformed or incomplete input.
caseStudy.results: Reduced repetitive manual operations and improved response consistency.
imageNotes: The project visual reflects the event-driven response behavior used in production tests.

title: MediTracker
description: Embedded monitoring system with Arduino and custom 3D printed enclosure.
contributionNote: Designed and 3D printed the enclosure and integrated Arduino firmware with hardware.

caseStudy.problem: Build an embedded monitor that is both technically reliable and physically manufacturable.
caseStudy.goal: Integrate firmware, electronics, and enclosure design into one coherent prototype.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: SolidWorks enclosure design + Arduino control logic + iterative print-and-test hardware loop.
workflow: Designed and revised enclosure geometry for board placement and wiring clearance.
Built firmware logic for sensing and control behavior.
Ran iterative fit checks and assembly tests across print revisions.
Finalized geometry and control logic for stable integrated operation.
caseStudy.results: Delivered a full electromechanical prototype with validated fit, assembly, and embedded behavior.
imageNotes: Image 1 shows early enclosure validation for volume and board fit.
Image 2 reflects revised structure for mounting and cable paths.
Image 3 highlights improved assembly geometry after tolerance correction.
Image 4 captures final integrated enclosure state.

title: 3D Printing
description: Custom-designed components supporting embedded system builds.
contributionNote: Produced functional parts for MediTracker and embedded prototypes through iterative print cycles.

caseStudy.problem: Embedded prototypes needed custom mechanical parts with fast turnaround.
caseStudy.goal: Create robust, fit-accurate printed components for real hardware integration.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: CAD modeling -> slicing configuration -> print -> fit validation -> design iteration.
workflow: Modeled functional parts around electronic component constraints.
Tuned print parameters for strength and surface quality.
Validated each revision by physical fit and integration checks.
caseStudy.results: Improved iteration speed and mechanical reliability across multiple builds.
imageNotes: Image 2 captures refined geometry for manufacturability.
Image 4 shows final usable component revision.

title: CityLight IQ
description: LabVIEW-based adaptive lighting system using block diagram logic and LED brightness control.
contributionNote: Implemented adaptive LED brightness logic and event-driven LabVIEW simulation blocks.

caseStudy.problem: Conventional lighting control lacks adaptive behavior for changing ambient conditions.
caseStudy.goal: Simulate a practical adaptive lighting model that scales LED output by light input state.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Ambient simulation input drives event-based LabVIEW block logic and multi-level LED control output.
workflow: Defined threshold states for ambient light ranges.
Built event-driven control logic in LabVIEW block diagrams.
Validated transition behavior across multiple simulated conditions.
caseStudy.results: Created a stable adaptive control simulation with clear system behavior and predictable response.

title: Magnetic Levitation
description: Control system designed in MATLAB & Simulink.
contributionNote: Group project - Designed compensator and control model.

caseStudy.problem: Magnetic levitation dynamics are inherently unstable and require careful control design.
caseStudy.goal: Stabilize the plant using compensator-based control in a model-driven workflow.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Plant model analysis in MATLAB with closed-loop compensator simulation in Simulink.
workflow: Analyzed plant behavior and defined control targets.
Designed compensator to improve stability margins.
Implemented and validated closed-loop behavior in Simulink.
caseStudy.results: Achieved stable simulated behavior and met key transient response expectations.

title: Fountain Nozzle
description: Mechanical nozzle design study with multiple SolidWorks variants for controlled flow patterns.
contributionNote: Translated concept inspiration into production-oriented CAD variants and assembly-ready models.

caseStudy.problem: Create nozzle geometries that can deliver different spray behaviors while remaining manufacturable.
caseStudy.goal: Develop and compare multiple nozzle concepts through a structured CAD iteration process.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Concept reference mapping -> part modeling -> assembly variants -> design comparison for manufacturability and flow intent.
workflow: Used concept reference to define initial geometry direction.
Built baseline nozzle architecture in SolidWorks.
Developed multiple assembly variants for different spray objectives.
Compared complexity, integration fit, and mechanical viability.
caseStudy.results: Produced a clear multi-variant mechanical design set ready for downstream prototyping decisions.
imageNotes: Image 1 is the original inspiration concept used to define geometry intent.
Assembly Dagi model shows one directional architecture with focused outlet behavior.
Assembly Multi model explores broader multi-output spray behavior.
Straight Jet model targets direct controlled flow through simplified outlet geometry.

title: Smart Miner Helmet
description: Embedded safety logic concept for miner protection using integrated sensor-driven alert behavior.
contributionNote: Designed embedded safety logic and integration strategy for practical field-oriented operation.

caseStudy.problem: Mining environments require rapid, reliable hazard detection and alert communication at the edge.
caseStudy.goal: Design a portable embedded safety logic framework for early hazard signaling and operator awareness.
caseStudy.systemArchitecture: Sensor input layer -> threshold and decision logic -> alert signaling outputs in real-time.
workflow: Defined safety events and threshold-driven logic states.
Designed embedded decision flow for fast hazard classification.
Mapped alert outputs for immediate operator feedback.
caseStudy.results: Established a practical safety-logic architecture that can be expanded into full hardware implementation.
imageNotes: Project currently uses a structured placeholder panel until dedicated design images are uploaded.
